_meta.description: Insight Gaps — BD-INV-002 Monthly Data Ledger. Tracks annual and monthly rape case filings, verdicts, and convictions in Bangladesh. Collection start point: August 2024. Everything before is covered by annual sources. CONFIRMED ABSENT entries are accountability failures, not research limitations. CC BY-NC 4.0.
_meta.note_on_methodology: PHQ Figure A (used here) includes completed rape + Section 9(4) attempt to rape under WCRPA. Studies using 'completed rape only' will show lower figures (e.g. 2019: 5,872 vs 6,766). All figures here use Figure A unless otherwise noted.

months.notes: Includes Section 9(4) attempt to rape. This is Figure A (PHQ total). Figure B (completed rape only) = 5,872.

notable_cases: Death penalty introduced Oct 2020 (Ordinance)
Begumganj video — nationwide protests
Sylhet MC College gang rape — nationwide protests
months.notes: Death penalty introduced October 2020 following mass protests. Figure B (ResearchGate/Ipas) gives 6,555 — higher than Figure A due to late-year protest-driven filing surge captured in different administrative cutoff. Discrepancy explained by methodology, not data error.

notable_cases: Raintree Hotel verdict and judge's 72-hour rule statement (Nov 2021) — documented chilling effect on 2022 filings
sources: University of Asia Pacific study May 2024 — S-62
months.notes: Two independent sources both cite PHQ data and agree on 6,341. Neither is the primary PHQ document. Tier will upgrade to CONFIRMED once primary PHQ annual report 2021 is located.

notable_cases: DNA lab manpower crisis — May 2022, 54 staff unpaid across 8 labs
25% drop from 2021 peak — three documented causes
sources: ResearchGate University of Asia Pacific study May 2024 — S-62
months.notes: 25% drop from 2021 explained by three documented causes: (1) DNA lab manpower transition crisis May 2022 — 54 scientific officers unpaid, 8 labs stalled; (2) Raintree Hotel judge 72-hour rule statement chilling effect; (3) death penalty paradox suppressing reporting of domestic perpetrators. NOTE: Prothom Alo (S-61) gives 6,032 for 2022 — this conflicts with ResearchGate figure of 4,762. Discrepancy under investigation. S-62 figure (4,762) used here as it is consistent with two academic corroborations. Do not upgrade tier until conflict resolved.
Standard annual PHQ data. 13/day average referenced in counter band.

notable_cases: Jan-Jul 2024: ZERO OCC convictions from 12 verdicts
Aug 2024: Political revolution — Awami League government fell
Aug 2024 onwards: 8,307 cases in 6 months (surge post-revolution)
sources: PHQ via Daily Star Mar 2025 — S-57
OCC data Daily Star Nov 2024 — S-55
months.notes: Annual total 4,394 confirmed. OCC data Jan-Jul 2024: 643 visitors, 227 cases filed, 12 verdicts, ZERO convictions. Post-August surge documented separately (Aug 2024-Jan 2025: 8,307 total cases per S-65 — this is WCRPA total, not rape-specific). 2024 tribunal throughput: 17,544 new cases filed vs 5,648 disposed — net backlog growth ~12,000 (S-65, PROBABLE).

notable_cases: Post-revolution — interim government took power Aug 8, 2024
Police system collapsed for several weeks after revolution
months.notes: PHQ does not publish monthly breakdowns. No August 2024 monthly figure exists in any public source. The political revolution of August 5, 2024 caused a reported surge in crime filings — but no monthly-disaggregated data is available. CONFIRMED ABSENT after exhaustive search.
PHQ does not publish monthly breakdowns. No September 2024 figure in any public source. CONFIRMED ABSENT.

notable_cases: Jan-Feb 2025 combined: 832 rape cases (PROBABLE — S-65)
sources: Prothom Alo crime analysis — S-65 (Jan-Feb combined only, not January alone)
months.notes: January figure cannot be disaggregated from Jan-Feb combined total of 832 cases (S-65). Monthly-specific figure: CONFIRMED ABSENT. Combined Jan-Feb figure is PROBABLE.

notable_cases: Jan-Feb 2025 combined: 832 rape cases (PROBABLE — S-65)
sources: Prothom Alo crime analysis — S-65 (Jan-Feb combined only)
months.notes: February figure cannot be disaggregated from Jan-Feb combined total. CONFIRMED ABSENT for monthly-specific.

notable_cases: Magura child rape case (MG-2025-001) — national crisis
WCRPA Amendment Ordinance passed March 20, 2025
148,314 backlog figure published — March 31, 2025
months.notes: Monthly case filing total not published. CONFIRMED ABSENT. Notable: March 2025 was the month that triggered the largest legislative reform in 25 years (2025 Ordinance) and established the Magura precedent.

notable_cases: Magura charge sheet filed April 13
Magura trial commenced April 23
months.notes: Monthly filing total: CONFIRMED ABSENT. PHQ does not publish monthly breakdowns.

notable_cases: Magura verdict May 17 — Hitu Sheikh death sentence (MG-2025-001)
months.notes: Monthly total filing: CONFIRMED ABSENT. However: May 17 verdict in Magura case (death sentence) is CONFIRMED. This is 1 confirmed conviction for this month from tracked cases — actual monthly national total unknown.
PHQ does not publish monthly breakdowns. CONFIRMED ABSENT.

notable_cases: Magura HC appeal admitted July 1 by Justice Mostafizur Rahman + Justice Sagir Hossain
months.notes: Monthly filing total: CONFIRMED ABSENT. HC appeal admission for Magura case CONFIRMED.

notable_cases: Daily Star (Sep 25, 2025) reported a figure of 10,475 in context of 2025 WCRPA statistics — exact category UNVERIFIED
months.notes: Monthly filing total: CONFIRMED ABSENT. A figure of 10,475 appeared in Daily Star Sep 25, 2025 in the context of 2025 WCRPA statistics — but exact category (total WCRPA vs rape-specific, cumulative vs monthly) requires original article verification before use. Currently UNVERIFIED for this purpose.

notable_cases: DNA backlog: 1,204 of 1,304 samples pending (92%) — Prothom Alo Sep 2025
months.notes: Monthly filing total: CONFIRMED ABSENT. DNA backlog figure (1,204/1,304) confirmed from this period.
PHQ does not publish monthly breakdowns. CONFIRMED ABSENT. Automated collector monitoring from this point forward.

notable_cases: Barishal Trish Godown verdict — 4 death sentences for 2016 gang rape (BS-2016-001)
months.notes: Monthly total filing: CONFIRMED ABSENT. However February 2026 Barishal verdict (4 death sentences, 2016 case) is CONFIRMED. A case from 2016 reached verdict in February 2026 — approximately 3,380 days.

notable_cases: Magura HC appeal ongoing as of March 2026
months.notes: Current month. Automated collector active. No monthly total published.
